To make your `saturation=30` in `mpv.conf` take effect, I'll update the drive-based script so it no longer forces saturation by default and only sets saturation if you explicitly provide it for a specific drive.

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="apply_patch"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **apply_patch**</summary>

**Input:**

- explanation: `Stop overriding global saturation from mpv.conf by default; only set saturation when explicitly provided for a drive, and do nothing for other drives. Also make volume optional and avoid applying defaults when drive not matched.`
- input:

```
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: c:\mega\Backups\mpv\portable_config\scripts\user-ai-w-drives.lua
@@
--- drive_specific_settings.lua
+-- drive_specific_settings.lua
@@
-local TARGET_DRIVES = {
-    -- Drive letter (uppercase) : {saturation, volume}
-    W = {saturation = 0.01, volume = 20},
-    X = {saturation = 0.01, volume = 20},
-    -- Add more drives here if needed, e.g.:
-    -- Y = {saturation = 10, volume = 50},
-}
+local TARGET_DRIVES = {
+    -- Drive letter (uppercase) : {saturation?, volume?}
+    -- By default, do NOT set saturation here so mpv.conf (e.g. saturation=30) is respected.
+    -- If you want a per-drive override, add `saturation = <value>` explicitly.
+    W = { volume = 20 },
+    X = { volume = 20 },
+    -- Add more drives here if needed, e.g.:
+    -- Y = { saturation = 10, volume = 50 },
+}
@@
--- Default settings to apply when the drive is NOT in TARGET_DRIVES
--- You might want to remove these if you prefer mpv's global defaults
-local DEFAULT_SETTINGS = {
-    saturation = 0.01, -- Default saturation
-    volume = 100       -- Default volume (or whatever your mpv.conf default is)
-}
+-- When a drive is NOT in TARGET_DRIVES, we now do NOTHING, so mpv.conf defaults are preserved.
+-- If you prefer to force some defaults regardless of drive, you can reintroduce a DEFAULT_SETTINGS
+-- table and assign it below when `settings_to_apply` is nil.
+-- local DEFAULT_SETTINGS = { volume = 100 }
@@
-    local settings_to_apply = nil
-
-    if drive_letter and TARGET_DRIVES[drive_letter] then
-        settings_to_apply = TARGET_DRIVES[drive_letter]
-        mp.msg.info(string.format("drive_specific_settings: Applying settings for drive %s.", drive_letter))
-    else
-        settings_to_apply = DEFAULT_SETTINGS
-        if drive_letter then
-            mp.msg.info(string.format("drive_specific_settings: Applying default settings (drive %s not in list).", drive_letter))
-        else
-            mp.msg.info("drive_specific_settings: Applying default settings (no drive letter found).")
-        end
-    end
-
-    if settings_to_apply then
-        mp.set_property("saturation", settings_to_apply.saturation)
-        mp.set_property("volume", settings_to_apply.volume)
-    end
+    local settings_to_apply = nil
+
+    if drive_letter and TARGET_DRIVES[drive_letter] then
+        settings_to_apply = TARGET_DRIVES[drive_letter]
+        mp.msg.info(string.format("drive_specific_settings: Applying settings for drive %s.", drive_letter))
+    else
+        -- Do nothing; preserve mpv.conf settings
+        if drive_letter then
+            mp.msg.info(string.format("drive_specific_settings: No per-drive settings for %s; leaving mpv.conf as-is.", drive_letter))
+        else
+            mp.msg.info("drive_specific_settings: No drive letter found; leaving mpv.conf as-is.")
+        end
+        return
+    end
+
+    -- Only set properties that are explicitly provided in the per-drive table
+    if settings_to_apply.saturation ~= nil then
+        mp.set_property("saturation", settings_to_apply.saturation)
+    end
+    if settings_to_apply.volume ~= nil then
+        mp.set_property("volume", settings_to_apply.volume)
+    end
 end
*** End Patch
```


</details></tool-use>
